Re: Request for Informal Advice
Greetings :
Antioch tilayor JoeI Ke1ler has authorized and
requested me to seek the FPPcrs opinion on the following
matter. A copy of your response can be nailed to D1ayor KeLler
at P. o. Box 130, Antioch, california 94509-0504, although I
would like either the original or a copy as $reLl.
Mayor Keller is a nenber of the Kaiser Permanente
system for health care. T$/o other members of the five-rnember
City Council are also nembers of Kaiser Permanente.
The city of Antioch offers Kaiser as one of three
health insurance plans for both ernployees and elected
officials Iike Mayor KeIIer. uayor Ke11er and the other
rnembers of the City Council may have Kaiser coverage either
through their positions with the City, or with their own
enployers.
A Kaiser Pemanente rnedical clinic facility 
is
currentl-y . located in Antioch. Kaiser is considering either
acquiring, or constructing, a hospital facility 
within the
city. 
If a ner./ facility 
is constructed, a number of land use
appiovals would be necessary frorn the City, including.Iand use
entittements which would have to be approved by the city
Council.

January 2, l99O
The nearest existing Kaiser hospitals to Antioch are
located in Martinez and walnut creek, both about 20 niles
distance fron Antioch.
Kaiser Pernanente menbers are entitled to emergency
treatnent at the existing private Detta Irlemorial Hospital in
Antioch should their injury or illness preclude them from
travelling to the Kaiser hospitals.
l,layor Keller inquires whether he can act on the land
use approvals for the Kaiser facility 
irrespectj,ve of the fact
that he is a Xaiser member. He also inquires what would
happen if the FPPC decides that there is a conflict, 
and the
two other Council members are sinilarly affected.
several city staff nembers who are atso Kaiser
members would also have dealings with the project, including
members of the planning staff, building inspection,
adninistrative services, etc. 
The l,layor also wonders whether
these ernployees would have a conflict.
f have opined to the uayor that there is no economic
benefit or detriment which would flow to himself, the other
members of the council, or to the staff, by virtue of the
location of the hospital in Antioch. There is the convenience
factor and slight savings of driving costs that would be
realized if a person did not have to travel to either Martinez
or walnut creek for hospital services. Holrever, these
benefits appear to be de ninirnis. r have also opined that any
benefit which would be received by the Mayor would be
identical to the benefit received by the public in general if
a Kaiser hospital is constructed in the city.
I have felt sonewhat unconfortable with rny opinion,
since I am a member of Kaiser nyself. 
Due to the potentially
widespread effect (i.e. a rnajority of the council, a large
nurnber of city employees) the tlayor desires the advice of your
office on this natter. 
Thank you.
very truly yours,

sections.question: Mayor Keller inquires whether he can act on the land use approvals for the Kaiser facility irrespective of the fact that he is a Kaiser member. He also inquires what would happen if the FPPC decides that there is a conflict, and the two other Council members are similarly affected. The Mayor also wonders whether city staff members who are Kaiser members would have a conflict.
sections.question_synthetic: Can Mayor Keller and two other City Council members participate in land use approval decisions for a Kaiser Permanente hospital facility when they are Kaiser members themselves, and would city staff with Kaiser membership face conflicts?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a City Attorney to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No conclusion/short answer section is present. The letter sets out the factual background and specific questions posed by the Mayor. The City Attorney also notes his own discomfort with his preliminary opinion due to being a Kaiser member himself.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Mayor Keller inquires whether he can act on the land use approvals for the Kaiser facility irrespective of the fact that he is a Kaiser member. He also inquires what would happen if the FPPC decides that there is a conflict, and the two other Council members are similarly affected. The Mayor also wonders whether city staff members who are Kaiser members would have a conflict.

embedding.summary: City Attorney William Gatstan requests FPPC advice on behalf of Antioch Mayor Joel Keller regarding whether the Mayor and other council members can participate in Kaiser Permanente facility approval decisions when they are Kaiser members and receive health insurance through the city.
